channel.title: City of Belvedere News
channel.description: Official news and updates from the City of Belvedere, California

item.title: Volunteer for the Planning Commission
item.description: The Belvedere City Council is now accepting letters of interest for an unexpected vacancy on the Planning Commission. This vacant seat has a term that ends on June 30, 2028. The Planning Commission reviews development applications for design review and zoning conformity in accordance with the Belvedere Municipal Code. Commissioners also serve outside of regular...

item.title: City Council Retreat: Shaping Belvedere’s 2026 Priorities
item.description: The Belvedere City Council held its Annual Retreat on February 23 to establish the city’s strategic goals and financial priorities for the coming year. Key discussions focused on maintaining fiscal health including a further review of user fees and pension funding, as well as advancing critical infrastructure projects. The Council also discussed a streamlined approach...

item.title: Belvedere Receives State Approval of Housing Element
item.description: On February 26, 2026, the city received a letter from the California Department of Housing & Development (HCD) confirming that Belvedere’s Housing Element is in compliance with State Housing Element Law. This milestone reflects several years of work by city staff and councilmembers to develop a plan that meets state requirements while thoughtfully addressing the...

item.title: Beach Road Oversight Subcommittee Update
item.description: The Beach Road Stabilization Project Oversight Subcommittee held its kick-off meeting on February 26. This citizen-led group has been tasked with monitoring the emergency repair project aimed at stabilizing the existing concrete seawall and preventing further settlement or displacement along a 580-foot section of Beach Road east of Peninsula Road. The subcommittee will review the...

item.title: MCE Takes Steps to Provide Rate Relief
item.description: MCE Clean Energy is a local public agency serving four counties, launched in 2010 to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and offer stable, cost-competitive electricity rates as a local alternative to PG&E. While MCE’s greenhouse gas emissions are currently similar to PG&E’s, MCE provides higher renewable energy content. Belvedere residents continue to have the choice between MCE and...

item.title: Help Make Belvedere #1 in Safety
item.description: For a community that often leads the way, it’s surprising that Belvedere ranks near the bottom—10th out of 12 Marin communities—when it comes to AlertMarin registrations. AlertMarin is Marin County's official emergency notification system. Belvedere Police Chief Jason Wu says it's our primary tool for reaching residents when it matters most. Whether it's a wildfire, flood...

item.title: Traffic Study: Addressing Traffic on Tiburon Boulevard
item.description: In 2025, the Tiburon Town Council initiated a study focused on Tiburon Boulevard in order to develop a comprehensive plan addressing traffic challenges and improving the Peninsula’s transportation network. On March 4, 2026, the Tiburon Town Council reviewed the first draft of a comprehensive Transportation & Infrastructure Study Plan. The study focuses on Tiburon Boulevard (from Blackfield Drive...

item.title: Learn about the Wildflowers of the Peninsula: March 25
item.description: The Tiburon Peninsula’s grasslands and woodlands are home to an impressive number of wildflowers, including several rare species. Join Shannon Burke of Marin County Parks on Wednesday, March 25 at 1pm in the Library's Founders room for a talk on the rare and common plants found here, the clever ways they attract pollinators, and the...

item.title: Spring Festival & Egg Hunt: Sunday, March 29
item.description: On Sunday, March 29 from 10am to 12:30pm, the Ranch will host its annual Spring Carnival and Egg Hunt at Belvedere Community Park. The day includes egg hunts for various ages, a petting zoo, bounce house, face painting, balloon twisters, and photo visits with the Bunny. Make sure to purchase your ticket based upon your age group....

item.title: Kemnitzer & Martori Honored as 2025 Citizens of the Year
item.description: At the Annual Town meeting on January 27, Belvedere honored two standout volunteers whose service has helped our community flourish. Our 2025 Citizen of the Year, Nancy Kemnitzer (pictured left) was recognized for more than 16 years of steady, thoughtful service on both the City Council and the Planning Commission. Her commitment has shaped many...

item.title: City Council Retreat: Monday, February 23 at Noon
item.description: The City Council Annual Retreat will be held on Monday, February 23, at the Belvedere Community Center, from noon to approximately 4pm. This planning session will focus on setting the city’s priorities, goals, and strategies for 2026. Community members are welcome to attend in person or via Zoom. Your interest and participation are always appreciated....

item.title: Parks, Open Spaces & Lanes Committee Update
item.description: Charlie Harrington, Chair of the Parks, Open Spaces and Lanes Committee (POSLC), presented the committee’s annual report to the City Council on February 9, highlighting several key accomplishments over the past year. Significant repairs and maintenance were completed on Cedar Lane, Park Lane, and Woodland Lane. In addition, the Lanes Subcommittee conducted a comprehensive assessment...

item.title: Registration is Open for the Ranch’s Summer Camps
item.description: The Ranch’s summer camps aren’t just day care; they offer immersive experiences into the great outdoors, worlds of magic, and so much more. The 2026 summer camp programs include: Angel Island Day Camps, Fantastical Adventures Day Camps, Counselor in Training Program, Art & Garden Camps, Musical Theater Camps, Lego Camps and Art Camps. Fee assistance...

item.title: Citywide Storm Drain Inspections
item.description: You may have noticed a van parked along city curbs in recent weeks with equipment extending into storm drains, catch basins, or manholes. Safe2Core, a city contractor, is conducting citywide storm drain Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) inspections. Using wheeled robotic cameras, technicians are recording the condition of Belvedere’s stormwater pipes as part of a broader...

item.title: Emergency Notification News
item.description: Start the new year safer! Now is the perfect time to replace the batteries in all older smoke and carbon monoxide detectors—make this an annual habit. Newer smoke and CO₂ detectors are sealed with 10-year batteries, so check the manufacture date to be sure yours are still within their lifespan. While you’re in safety mode,...

item.title: Aftercare Bus Spring Passes on Sale March 3
item.description: Bus passes for the Ranch's after-school program will go on sale for the spring session on Tuesday, March 3, at 9am. Visit the Tiburon Peninsula Traffic Alliance webpage for more information and to purchase passes, which are sold by season and individual day, for $3 per ride.

item.title: Traffic Talk: Tiburon Traffic Study Results March 4
item.description: Curious about what’s next for traffic, safety, and getting around the peninsula? On March 4 at 6:30pm at Tiburon Town Hall, the Tiburon Town Council will review the results of the Tiburon Traffic Study, commissioned in 2024 to evaluate key corridors, including Tiburon Boulevard. The report recommends ways to reduce congestion, improve safety, and boost...

item.title: Seed Swap & Plant Exchange: Saturday, February 21
item.description: Get expert advice from Marin Master Gardeners on seed saving, pollinator plants, planting your spring and summer garden, and more. On February 21 at 11am in the Library Founders’ Room, enjoy hands-on, make-and-take projects like planting in biodegradable pots, creating seed tape and rolling seed balls. Bring seeds you’ve harvested or plant cuttings to share with the...

item.title: City Manager Report
item.description: Thanks for pausing to read the Belvedere Breeze! The City Council met this week to discuss the city’s budget, with a particular focus on how to thoughtfully allocate $1,030,000 in excess reserves for one-time operational and capital needs. Council approved $200,000 for several important projects—updates to Police Department facilities, debris removal along the Beach Road shoreline, and...

item.title: Keep Your Dog on the Right Side of the Law
item.description: Making sure your dog is licensed not only helps keep them safe - it's also required by law. Marin Humane is offering a new, easy-to-use licensing platform that provides effective pet identification and reunification services, helping keep pets out of shelters and return them home safely. Visit Marin Humane to purchase a new license or renew an existing one....
